Check: Is this the same value divided by same value? If so we should be doing live vix / prior day close?
TARS Response: NO, not the same. VIX1D measures 1-day expected vol, VIX measures 30-day expected vol. Different indices. The ratio VIX1D/VIX > 1 means near-term fear elevated vs longer-term. Currently both use same-day close = LEAK. FIX: after shifting both to prior day, this becomes prior_day_vix1d / prior_day_vix. For V2 intraday: could use live VIX1D / live VIX from ThetaData.

Check: Is this the same as first_15m_return_pct?
TARS Response: NO, different. spx_ret_5m = SPX return over the TRAILING 5m from the current minute. This changes every minute. At 10:15, spx_ret_5m = return from 10:10 to 10:15. first_15m_return_pct = return from 9:30 to 9:45, FIXED for the day (doesnt change). spx_ret_* are INTRADAY features that slide with time. first_*m are DAILY features computed once.
Train Source: SPX 1-min bars. (close_T1 - close_T6) / close_T6 * 100. All completed bars. Uses T-1 close as current price (training matches production).
Production Source: ThetaData SPX 1-min bars. Same formula from accumulated completed bars.
Storage: spx_1min.parquet — computed from bars appended live to parquet
Production Computation: (T-1 close - T-6 close) / T-6 close * 100. | UNIVERSAL RULE: All features use T-1 completed 1-min bar data. Production waits for bar close, fetches from ThetaData.

Check: Is this the same as first_15m_return_pct?

At 10:15, spx_ret_5m = return from 10:10 to 10:15. first_15m_return_pct = return from 9:30 to 9:45, FIXED for the day (doesnt change). spx_ret_* are INTRADAY features that slide with time. first_*m are DAILY features computed once.
Train Source: SPX 1-min bars. (close_T1 - close_T11) / close_T11 * 100. All completed bars. Uses T-1 close as current price (training matches production).
Production Source: ThetaData SPX 1-min bars. Same formula from accumulated completed bars.
